proved: Strict public profile and certification schemas reject unknown fields.

The semantic validator rejects built-in provider shadowing, protocol-route mismatch, non-literal local hosts, remote HTTP, missing authentication, false local-only remote storage, and missing dated remote policy evidence.
Endpoint probes allow only the declared origin and route and deny alternate routes, ports, queries, fragments, and embedded credentials.
The package certifier bounds JSON, rejects traversal, requires documentation/license plus expected-valid and expected-invalid profiles, and requires denied endpoint probes.
The interface-only local-vLLM example passes contract certification without an endpoint call.
The compiled MCP embeds the same strict schemas and reports the certifier as contract-certified-runner while custom execution remains contract-only.
notProved: The adapter can contact vLLM or any other endpoint.
Responses, Chat Completions, or Messages wire compatibility.
Streaming, tools, images, structured output, usage, or model quality.
Credential storage or product UI integration.
Safety of arbitrary adapter code or protocols; no adapter code executes in this certification tier.
